sourceBackedRoles: Activity progress is visually emphasized in the captured Summary Dashboard screen.
A circular progress visual and current goal-relative metric are source-observed.
Progress color is meaning-bearing for the Activity core, but exact state colors are not reviewed.
runtimeAssumptions: Completed, low, empty, overachieved, and unavailable progress colors are runtime validation assumptions.
The local ProgressRing color treatment is not a source token value.
Activity color roles should not be treated as accepted raw values.
reviewedGroups.candidateDecision: Keep the color.progress group as a semantic candidate; defer raw values and state-specific color acceptance.
candidateTokenActions: Keep color.progress.pending as source-backed in role only.
Keep color.progress.completed, low, empty, overachieved, and unavailable as runtime-tested candidates.
Defer any split between source activity color and web runtime progress-state colors until source value review.
reviewedGroups.nextAction: Review source screenshots or variables for progress state separation before extracting raw values.

sourceBackedRoles: A Trends section and Trends Box are source-observed in the captured Summary Dashboard screen.
Trend is source-backed as an interpretation module in the SummaryDashboard concept loop.
Action emphasis is visible in the source, but direction-specific colors are not source-reviewed.
runtimeAssumptions: Improving, declining, neutral, insufficient-data, and default trend colors are synthetic runtime states.
The local TrendCard status palette is not source visual evidence.
No chart, sparkline, arrow-heavy, or raw color treatment is promoted from source.
reviewedGroups.candidateDecision: Keep the color.trend group as a semantic candidate; defer direction-color acceptance until source states are observed or reviewed.
candidateTokenActions: Keep color.trend.default for the source-observed trend module role.
Keep color.trend.positive, negative, neutral, and insufficientData as runtime-only candidates.
Defer any merge with state.unavailable until insufficient-data source behavior is reviewed.
reviewedGroups.nextAction: Collect source-backed trend direction and insufficient-data evidence before accepting trend colors.

sourceBackedRoles: An Awards section and repeated award items are source-observed in the captured Summary Dashboard screen.
Reward evidence exists as an achievement preview module.
Award titles and progress-like evidence are visible, but status-specific source colors are not reviewed.
runtimeAssumptions: Locked, unlocked, new, and in-progress reward colors are synthetic runtime validation states.
Placeholder symbols and local award colors are not source icon or token evidence.
Decoration-only reward styling remains rejected as system evidence.
reviewedGroups.candidateDecision: Keep the color.reward group as a semantic candidate; defer status-specific raw values and source-state acceptance.
candidateTokenActions: Keep color.reward.default as the source-observed reward module role.
Keep color.reward.locked, unlocked, new, and inProgress as runtime-tested candidates.
Defer real status-color review until award source states are captured or explicitly reviewed.
reviewedGroups.nextAction: Review source-backed award status meaning before extracting or accepting reward colors.

sourceBackedRoles: Activity metric label, value, unit, and supporting metric hierarchy are visible in captured source evidence.
The source separates current progress value from supporting Steps and Distance values.
Metric typography roles are strongly source-backed as semantic hierarchy, not raw font values.
runtimeAssumptions: Exact font family, size, weight, line-height, and letter spacing remain runtime CSS assumptions.
Localization and extreme-value handling remain Storybook runtime validation, not source visual evidence.
Metric color and typography raw values are not extracted.
reviewedGroups.candidateDecision: Keep typography.metric candidates; treat value/unit/label/caption roles as source-backed semantic roles with pending raw values.
candidateTokenActions: Keep typography.metric.value, unit, label, and caption as distinct candidates.
Do not merge metric typography into generic card typography.
Defer raw font values and responsive type scaling rules.
reviewedGroups.nextAction: Review source typography metadata or variables before any raw typography token extraction.

sourceBackedRoles: Workout rows show completed-record title, duration, and date metadata in captured source evidence.
Record typography is distinct from compact metric display.
The title and metadata hierarchy supports WorkoutItem as completed behavior record.
runtimeAssumptions: Missing duration/date typography is synthetic runtime validation.
Exact record font values and metadata color values are not source-reviewed.
Interactive target typography remains web adaptation behavior.
reviewedGroups.candidateDecision: Keep typography.record candidates; keep record title and metadata separated from MetricCard typography.
candidateTokenActions: Keep typography.record.title and metadata as source-backed semantic roles.
Keep typography.record.label and unavailable as runtime candidate roles.
Do not merge record typography into typography.metric.
reviewedGroups.nextAction: Review source typography metadata for workout records before extracting raw font values.

sourceBackedRoles: The captured Summary Dashboard shows section order and broad vertical rhythm across Activity, Workouts, Trends, and Awards.
Summary spacing supports scan order and optional module previewing.
Source evidence confirms spacing relationships only qualitatively.
runtimeAssumptions: Exact padding, section gap, module gap, and header gap are local runtime CSS assumptions.
Desktop/web adaptation spacing does not come from source evidence.
Spacing values are not extracted or accepted.
reviewedGroups.candidateDecision: Keep spacing.summary candidates as semantic spacing roles; defer raw spacing values.
candidateTokenActions: Keep spacing.summary.padding, sectionGap, moduleGap, and headerGap as separate candidates.
Do not collapse summary spacing into component spacing yet.
Defer numeric spacing values and breakpoint behavior.
reviewedGroups.nextAction: Review source layout metadata and responsive runtime constraints before accepting spacing tokens.

sourceBackedRoles: The source Summary Dashboard is a compact daily review surface with multiple modules visible in one scroll flow.
Density supports quick scanning across Goal, Record, Trend, and Reward evidence.
Source evidence backs the density direction but not transferable density values.
runtimeAssumptions: CoreOnly, composed, and modulePreview density roles are runtime validation scaffolding.
Web viewport density is not source visual evidence.
No density scale or numeric density value is accepted.
reviewedGroups.candidateDecision: Keep density.dashboard candidates; defer density scale acceptance until source and transferability review.
candidateTokenActions: Keep density.dashboard.compact, composed, and modulePreview.
Do not promote dashboard density as a design-system rule yet.
Defer migration experiments for habit, learning, focus, or training products.
reviewedGroups.nextAction: Test source-backed dashboard density against transferability and accessibility before promotion.

sourceBackedRoles: Captured source evidence includes low or incomplete-looking progress and reward preview content, but not a confirmed unavailable-state system.
Insufficient, missing, empty, and unavailable states are mostly runtime-derived.
Source evidence does not yet establish a stable unavailable visual treatment.
runtimeAssumptions: Missing trend data, missing award data, missing workout metadata, and unavailable progress are synthetic runtime states.
Unavailable text, surface, border, and indicator roles are not source-reviewed.
Runtime CSS muted treatments are rejected as source evidence.
reviewedGroups.candidateDecision: Keep state.unavailable candidates as necessary runtime roles; do not treat them as source-backed visual tokens yet.
candidateTokenActions: Keep state.unavailable.text, surface, border, and indicator as candidates.
Defer source-backed separation between empty, unavailable, insufficient, and missing.
Do not merge state.unavailable into color.surface or color.trend until source behavior is clearer.
reviewedGroups.nextAction: Capture or review explicit source unavailable and insufficient-data states before accepting these tokens.

notes: Second twentieth-cycle execution review. It uses existing captured source evidence only and does not extract raw values, update CSS, or promote tokens.
